rationale: axon.query (cypher+parameters) and per-named-query tools generated from schema.queries; descriptions surface schema doc; both delegate to shared axon-graphql dynamic helpers preserving policy/limits/timeout. Wired into stdio and HTTP MCP. New tests cover happy-path and error cases.

findings.summary: execute_query still accepts a legacy 'query' string fallback after the cypher branch, but the tool input_schema requires 'cypher' so the GraphQL fallback path is unreachable through the registered MCP tool. Harmless but dead code.

build_named_query_tools_tokio uses handler.blocking_lock() during registry construction; this is fine at startup but would deadlock if ever called from inside a Tokio runtime worker without a current task. Today only called from setup paths.
